title: Beta — A note on bounded interfaces
description: Generic placeholder fixture exercising the lite editorial-piece primitive sequence (image hero + heading + body + pull quote + body).

sections.title: When the substrate is the seam
sections.lede: Most platforms widen their surface to absorb every use case. The result is a vocabulary nobody fully holds.
sections.photo_alt: Library shelving stacked floor to ceiling — illustrative.

sections.text: Start by listing every primitive the substrate exposes. Count them. Most platforms find a number between thirty and a hundred, and most operators recognize only a small fraction by name.
A bounded surface is not a poverty. It is a contract you can keep.

Build a narrower surface that exposes only the primitives an operator can hold in working memory. Watch what gets built. The signal arrives quickly — either the bounded surface produces better output, or it produces the same convergent outputs and the bottleneck lives elsewhere.
